title: Eupacannol, a pentacyclic triterpene of a new skeletal type, and other chemical constituents from Eupatorium cannabinum
author: Sunil Kumar Talapatra
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, University College of Science, University of Calcutta, Kolkata-700 009, India 

 E-mail: talapatrask@yahoo.com, talapatrask@vsnl.net, banitalapatra@yahoo.co.in 

  Manuscript received 10 December 2003  

 A pentacyclic triterpene, designated eupacannol (1), possessing a new skeletal type, has been isolated from the whole plant of  Eupatorium cannabinum  Linn. (Fam. Asteraceae) in 0.0055% yield. Its structure having a new modified friedlane skeleton has been established as 1 on the basis of  1 H NMR (using paramagnetic shift reagent also) and mass spectral evidence, and chemical studies. β-Amyrin and an anthraquinone derivative physcion have also been isolated. The molecular conformations of eupllcannol (I) and friedelan-7β-ol (5) have been discussed.
